data.title: Avecho Exceeds Phase III Insomnia Trial Target as Key Interim Read-Out Nears
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Avecho Biotechnology Limited ( (AU:AVE) ) has issued an announcement.

Avecho Biotechnology has surpassed its recruitment target for the interim analysis of its pivotal Phase III trial of a TPM-enhanced CBD soft-gel capsule for insomnia, randomising 244 patients, 16% above plan, to strengthen statistical power. The multi-centre, double-blind, placebo-controlled study, the largest CBD insomnia trial in Australia, is progressing to an interim data read-out in June 2026 under oversight from an independent Data Monitoring Board.

The company has positioned itself for commercialisation ahead of the read-out, with Sandoz AG holding Australian rights under a 10-year licensing deal that includes upfront and milestone payments plus tiered royalties. With new patent allowances in the U.S. and Europe and an Australian OTC CBD market forecast to exceed US$125 million annually within a multi-billion-dollar global insomnia segment, a positive interim result could significantly de-risk the program and accelerate Avecho’s path to market and partnering in territories outside Australia.

More about Avecho Biotechnology Limited

Avecho Biotechnology Limited is an Australian pharmaceutical company focused on developing TPM-enhanced cannabinoid formulations, including a cannabidiol (CBD) soft-gel capsule. The company targets the insomnia and broader sleep-disorder market, aiming to register its CBD product as an over-the-counter Schedule 3 medicine in Australia and to expand into major global markets.

Average Trading Volume: 6,215,137

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$40.41M

data.title: Ore Resources launches 15,000m aircore campaign at Coolgardie gold projects
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Auroch Minerals Ltd. ( (AU:OR3) ) has shared an announcement.

Ore Resources has commenced a 15,000m regional aircore drilling programme at its Coolgardie Gold Projects in Western Australia, marking a key step in its Phase 4 exploration strategy. The initial focus is the Burbanks East Gold Project, where first‑pass drilling will test five large, structurally controlled targets along a 2.7km gold‑in‑soil anomaly adjacent to the MacPhersons Reward mine, an area that has seen limited modern exploration but has returned promising historical intercepts.

The programme will then move to high‑priority prospects at the Miriam Gold Project, including Jungle, Forrest North, Burbanks Monarch, Goroke and systematic follow‑up along the 1.7km Canyon anomaly, where prior RC drilling has already confirmed a fertile gold system. Assay results, expected from late May 2026, will shape follow‑up RC, diamond and additional aircore drilling in the second half of 2026, with the campaign positioned to unlock new high‑grade discoveries, expand resources and strengthen Ore’s strategic foothold in the WA Goldfields.

More about Auroch Minerals Ltd.

Ore Resources Ltd is an Australian gold exploration company focused on advancing its Coolgardie and Kal East Gold Projects in Western Australia’s Goldfields region. The company targets structurally controlled and intrusion-related gold systems and is currently well funded, with A$10.7 million in cash and no debt as at 31 December 2025, supporting an extensive 2026 exploration programme.

YTD Price Performance: 17.24%

Average Trading Volume: 986,039

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$56.83M

data.title: FMR Resources Wraps Phase I Drilling at Chile JV as Assays Confirm Mineralised Systems in Chile and Canada
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Applyflow Limited ( (AU:FMR) ) has issued an update.

FMR Resources reported that Phase I drilling has been completed at the Southern Porphyry target area within the Llahuin Project joint venture in Chile, where multiple porphyry intrusive phases with visible copper and molybdenite sulphides indicate the outer zones of a large mineralised system. The company is now consolidating data to design a Phase II drill program, while initial assays from the Goshen Prospect at its Fairfield Project in Canada confirm the presence of a mineralised system, and it ended the quarter with cash of $3.077 million, underpinning ongoing exploration activities.

More about Applyflow Limited

FMR Resources Limited is an Australia-based mineral exploration company focused on copper, gold and molybdenite projects. The company’s key assets include the Llahuin copper-gold-molybdenite joint venture in central Chile and the Fairfield Project in New Brunswick, Canada, positioning it within the global base and precious metals exploration sector.

data.title: Chinney Alliance Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Chinney Alliance Group Limited ( (HK:0385) ) has issued an announcement.

Chinney Alliance Group Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 3 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider a proposed final dividend. The agenda also includes re-election of directors, re-appointment of the auditor, approval of directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, and the granting of a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, which could provide additional financial flexibility and potential funding options.

The share issuance mandate, subject to customary exclusions for rights issues, employee share schemes and scrip dividends, will remain effective until the next annual general meeting or earlier revocation by shareholders. This proposed authority underscores the company’s intention to maintain access to equity capital markets for future corporate or strategic needs, while leaving ultimate oversight with shareholders through periodic renewal of the mandate.

More about Chinney Alliance Group Limited

Chinney Alliance Group Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates as a diversified group through its subsidiaries. The company is part of the Hong Kong market and is subject to the corporate governance and disclosure requirements of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

data.title: Salmon Evolution Raises NOK 411 Million in Share Issues and Plans Subsequent Offering
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Salmon Evolution ASA ( (DE:60E) ) has provided an update.

Salmon Evolution ASA, a Norwegian land-based salmon farming specialist, is expanding from its operational hub at Indre Harøy on the country’s west coast, leveraging its hybrid flow-through technology to scale production in the global aquaculture market. Listed on Oslo Børs under ticker SALME, the company positions itself as a biology-focused producer aiming to mitigate operational and biological risk while growing its footprint in salmon farming.

The company has completed a private placement and a retail offering, raising a total of NOK 411.3 million through the issuance of 91.4 million new shares at NOK 4.50 each. Board members and senior management participated significantly in the private placement, and following the capital increase Salmon Evolution’s share capital will rise to NOK 27.7 million, with 554,003,306 shares outstanding.

Settlement of the new shares is expected in early May 2026, subject to pre-funding arrangements and registration of the capital increase with the Norwegian authorities and the VPS. The board also plans to propose a subsequent offering of up to 11,111,111 additional shares, potentially raising about NOK 50 million for eligible shareholders who were not allocated shares in the private placement, underscoring an effort to balance rapid financing with equal treatment considerations.

The most recent analyst rating on (DE:60E) stock is a Buy with a NOK6.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Salmon Evolution ASA stock, see the DE:60E Stock Forecast page.

More about Salmon Evolution ASA

Salmon Evolution ASA is a global leader in land-based salmon farming, using its proprietary hybrid flow-through system to combine benefits of land and sea-based aquaculture while prioritizing biological risk management. The company operates its first large-scale facility and global centre of excellence at Indre Harøy on Norway’s west coast and is listed on Oslo Børs under the ticker SALME, targeting significant further expansion in the aquaculture industry.

Average Trading Volume: 758,197

Current Market Cap: NOK2.26B

data.title: Westgold Posts Strong Cash Build as Higginsville Expansion and Asset Deals Bolster Growth
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Westgold Resources ( (AU:WGX) ) just unveiled an update.

Westgold Resources reported a strong March quarter, producing 93,145 ounces of gold and maintaining FY26 guidance, though costs are expected near the top of the range amid inflation and deliberate cashflow-focused decisions. Revenue reached $495 million on gold sales of 69,900 ounces, supporting an underlying cash build of $285 million and lifting total cash, bullion and liquid investments to $856 million, with the company remaining debt-free and unhedged.

Operational performance improved at Bluebird–South Junction and Beta Hunt, where higher mining rates and the resolution of ventilation constraints are expected to lift output toward year-end. Open pit mining in the Murchison restarted three months early to improve ore blends and mill utilisation, while a board-approved expansion of the Higginsville plant in the Southern Goldfields is set to increase processing capacity, lower unit costs and support higher future gold production.

Westgold continued to streamline its portfolio by divesting the Mt Henry–Selene project and spinning out the Reedy and Comet assets, crystallising about $140 million in immediate value while retaining upside through equity stakes. Balance sheet flexibility was further strengthened through a larger unsecured credit facility, ongoing share buybacks and robust treasury, helping the group manage market volatility and fuel supply risks as it marked its elevation into the ASX 100.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WGX) stock is a Buy with a A$10.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Westgold Resources stock, see the AU:WGX Stock Forecast page.

More about Westgold Resources

Westgold Resources is an ASX100-listed Australian gold producer operating four mining hubs with about 6Mtpa of processing capacity across Western Australia’s Murchison and Southern Goldfields, two of the country’s most prolific gold regions. The company focuses on safe, responsible and profitable gold production, with key cornerstone assets including the Bluebird–South Junction and Beta Hunt underground mines underpinning its growth strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 5,310,276

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$5.74B

The latest announcement is out from Thunderbird Resources ( (AU:XPD) ).

Xpedra Resources has disclosed a change in director Gary Billingsley’s holdings, with his performance rights position materially revised following shareholder approval at an April general meeting. The director’s previous 600,000 performance rights expired due to unmet conditions, while 5,000,000 new performance rights were issued at no consideration, leaving him with 2,985,714 ordinary shares and 5,000,000 performance rights overall.

The transaction, which did not involve any cash payment, aligns Billingsley’s incentives more closely with shareholder outcomes through a larger at-risk equity component. For investors, the shift underscores the company’s continued use of performance-based equity to retain and motivate key leadership, while clarifying that the change occurred outside any trading blackout period and does not reflect on-market buying or selling activity.

More about Thunderbird Resources

Xpedra Resources Limited, which is referenced in the filing as the entity lodging the notice, operates in the resources sector. The company is listed on the ASX and its securities include ordinary shares and performance rights issued to directors as part of their remuneration and incentive structures, reflecting a focus on equity-based incentives for key management.

YY Group Holding Limited Class A ( (YYGH) ) has provided an announcement.

YY Group Holding Limited, a Singapore-based foreign private issuer of Class A ordinary shares in the U.S. market, maintains its primary listing framework via Form 20-F reporting. Its capital strategy relies on accessing U.S. investors through registered equity offerings, reflecting a cross-border financing model anchored in Singapore operations and U.S. regulatory oversight.

On April 28, 2026, YY Group relaunched its previously paused at-the-market equity offering of up to $20 million in Class A ordinary shares with Spartan Capital Securities and Wilson-Davis & Co. acting as sales agents. Following the March 30, 2026 pause and the relaunch, the company reported approximately $16.5 million of remaining capacity under the agreement, signaling continued use of the program to raise incremental capital as needed.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, YYGH is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial quality: despite strong revenue growth, recent large losses and negative operating/free cash flow materially increase risk.
